content:
Let $P=\{17,23\}$ be the prime divisors of $391=17\cdot23$.  For a pair $(a,b)$ with $a<391$, we want
   lcm$(a,b)>\,$lcm$(a,391)$.  Writing out prime‐factor exponents shows this forces two things:

1.  Every prime $q\in P$ must divide at least one of $a$ or $b$, so that no “extra” factor from $391$ remains in the denominator when comparing the two lcms.

2.  $b$ must contribute at least one prime factor not in $P$, so that the lcm with $b$ is strictly larger.

Hence $b$ must be divisible by at least one of $17,23$ and also by some other prime.  The smallest such $b$ is 
   $2\cdot17=34.$ 

For example, take $a=23<391$.  Then
   lcm$(23,34)=782$ while lcm$(23,391)=391$,
   and indeed $782>391$.  No smaller $b$ works.

Thus the minimum possible $b$ is \boxed{34}.
